symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe recurrence of right scrotal pain 4 days post-orchidopexy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden severe scrotal pain is torsion until proven otherwise; cannot be excluded remotely.
Rising intrascrotal pressure from a collection stretches the capsule and reproduces severe pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid right hemiscrotal swelling ('size of a lemon') with overlying erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid postoperative swelling and induration most often reflect a mechanical collection.
Erythema can also reflect early infection; not distinguishable from reactive change without exam.
Low-grade fever is non-specific early postoperatively; rigors would raise concern for sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild nausea secondary to pain, no vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nausea accompanies severe visceral scrotal pain of any cause.
Absence of urinary symptoms slightly lowers the probability of an ascending urinary-source infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wound clean and closed, no purulent discharge (parent's naked-eye view)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clean non-purulent wound lowers but does not exclude infection; deep assessment still needs a clinician.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense, exquisitely tender, highly indurated right hemiscrotum on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Generalised induration of the tissue planes rather than an isolated high-riding ischaemic testicle points to a reactive collection — a clinician-only palpation finding, sealed from the AI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Testicle palpable and fixed in the dependent scrotum; cremasteric reflex absent (post-fixation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A secure dependent testicle without a high-riding ischaemic lie makes recurrent torsion less likely — but this is a hands-on finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stable vital signs, not systemically toxic (BP 125/75, HR 98, Temp 37.6C, RR 16, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of tachycardia/high fever argues against established sepsis; clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Colour-Doppler scrotal ultrasound: normal robust symmetrical bilateral testicular arterial flow

investigation_details.expected_result: Preserved robust symmetrical bilateral testicular arterial flow, ruling out recurrent ischaemia; quantifies the haematoma/effusion.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Recurrent torsion / ischaemic compromise of the salvaged testicle cannot be excluded, risking testicular loss.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Demonstrated arterial flow is the definitive test that excludes ongoing ischaemia — unavailable in telehealth, hence the escalation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Recurrent testicular torsion / ischaemic compromise (to be excluded)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden severe pain plus rapid tense swelling in a post-orchidopexy scrotum defines a time-critical, exam-and-imaging-dependent problem that telehealth cannot clear.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitively assesses testicular arterial flow and quantifies the collection; arranged via in-person assessment, not by the AI.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person surgical/urology examination of the scrotum and wound
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Palpation of the induration, testicular lie and wound integrity — the assessment the AI cannot perform and the reason for escalation.
Infection is not established; antibiotics must not be minted remotely and would not address a haematoma or torsion — the in-person assessment decides.
